name: Edit-in-Place Intent
summary: The display‚Üíedit‚Üícommit/cancel lifecycle as a reusable thin primitive (placement ratified #1397): turn a displayed value into an editor in place and back ‚Äî click-to-edit field, editable table cell, rename-in-tree. Owns ONLY the thin residual ‚Äî the activation gesture, the display-renderer‚áÑeditor-renderer swap for one atomic value, and commit / cancel-revert-to-baseline (adopting the WAI-ARIA APG Enter/F2/Esc vocabulary) ‚Äî and COMPOSES input (the editor), validation (the commit gate), focus-delegation (cell focus), optionally rich-text (flowed editor) rather than re-owning them. The lifecycle recurs without a grid, so data-grid is the first consumer, not the home. Mirrors accessible-name (#596): own the thin recurring policy.

activation.description: The gesture that swaps the read-only display for the editor. `click` (single-click, the field default) ¬∑ `dblclick` (the cell default per APG) ¬∑ `affordance` (an explicit edit button/icon) ¬∑ `programmatic` (the host opens it, e.g. on row-add). Open-numbered; most-permissive default is the union the consumer picks ‚Äî a sensible single default is `click` for standalone fields and `dblclick`/F2 for grid cells.

commitOn.description: When the edited value is written back (the lifecycle's commit trigger). `blur` (focus leaves the editor) ¬∑ `enter` (Enter key) ¬∑ `explicit` (an Ok button / confirm affordance). Composes ‚Äî does not duplicate ‚Äî validation: this dimension fires the WRITE-BACK; whether that write is permitted is validation's `execution`/`commitment`. Default for fields is `blur`+`enter`; cells typically `enter`.

cancelOn.description: How an edit is abandoned, reverting the value to its baseline (the snapshot taken at activation). `escape` is adopted from the WAI-ARIA APG and is effectively FIXED ‚Äî Escape always reverts; the dimension exists to name the baseline-revert contract, not to offer alternatives.

editor.description: Which editor the edit view renders ‚Äî a COMPOSITION POINTER, not a new mechanism. `input` (a single-line/select input ‚Äî the default) ¬∑ `rich-text` (a flowed rich-text surface, composing the rich-text intent's editing mode) ¬∑ `custom` (a host-supplied editor element). The intent points at the editor; it never re-implements text entry.

description:
What This Is

The display→edit→commit/cancel lifecycle as a reusable thin primitive (placement ratified in #1397): turn a displayed value into an editor in place and back ‚Äî click-to-edit field, editable table cell, rename-in-tree. The lifecycle recurs without a grid (Atlassian @atlaskit/inline-edit, PrimeReact Inplace, tree-rename), so data-grid is the first consumer, not the home.

Owns the thin residual ‚Äî composes the rest

This intent owns only: the activation gesture, the display-renderer→editor-renderer swap for one atomic value, and commit / cancel-revert-to-baseline (the APG Enter / F2 / Esc vocabulary). It composes ‚Äî and never re-owns ‚Äî input (the editor), validation (the commit gate), focus-delegation (cell focus), and optionally rich-text (a flowed editor). Re-owning text entry or validation would duplicate/drift shipped standards (native-first). It sits at the accessible-name altitude ‚Äî a thin recurring policy, not a mega-widget.

Dimensions (draft)

  • activation: click / dblclick / affordance / programmatic ‚Äî how the swap is triggered.
  • commitOn: blur / enter / explicit ‚Äî when the write-back fires.
  • cancelOn: escape (revert to baseline) ‚Äî adopted from APG, effectively fixed.
  • editor: input / rich-text / custom ‚Äî a composition pointer to the edit view.

commitOn vs validation ‚Äî the one-sentence boundary

edit-in-place's commitOn decides when the write-back is attempted (blur / Enter / explicit), while validation's execution/commitment decides whether that attempt is permitted ‚Äî the gesture fires the commit, validation gates it, so they never both "own commit on blur."

Fixed a11y vocabulary ‚Äî APG

Borrowed from the WAI-ARIA APG: Enter or F2 enters edit mode (per the consumer's activation), Escape reverts to baseline, Enter commits. Cell focus during the swap is delegated to focus-delegation, so focus survives the renderer swap.

Realization

The contract (this intent) and its realization (a FUI behavior block + demo + data-grid editable-cell wiring) coexist at different layers ‚Äî the same intent+block split data-transfer/reorder/selection each ship. Grounded in the lifecycle survey (edit-in-place lifecycle placement).
